factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is not explicitly mentioned as growing, stable, or shrinking.

evidence.text: The text does not provide specific information on the growth or decline of order backlog or book-to-bill.

factors.summary: The company is shifting to a subscription model, which may indicate a strategic pricing approach rather than explicit price changes.

evidence.text: This impacts liquidity in a commensurate fashion, with the cash for the TASER 60 arrangement received in five annual installments rather than up front. It is our strategic intent to shift an increasing amount of our business to a subscription model.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards subscription models, which may indicate a strategic shift rather than a direct shift to higher or lower margin segments.

evidence.text: It is our strategic intent to shift an increasing amount of our business to a subscription model, to better match the municipal budgeting process of our customers as well as to allow for multiple product offerings to be bundled into existing subscriptions.

factors.summary: Organic growth is expected to accelerate due to the shift towards subscription models and new product launches.

evidence.text: With the launch of the TASER 7, which is primarily being sold in subscription offerings, we expect this strategic shift to accelerate.

factors.summary: The company is exposed to potential risks in raw material costs, particularly related to petroleum-based products and transportation.

evidence.text: A significant number of our raw materials or components are comprised of petroleum-based products or incur some form of landed cost associated with transporting the raw materials or components to our facility. Our freight and import costs and the timely delivery of our products could be adversely impacted by a number of factors which could reduce the profitability of our operations, including: higher fuel costs; potential port closures; customs clearance issues; increased government regulation or changes for imports of foreign products into the U.S.; delays created by terrorist attacks or threats, public health issues, national disasters or work stoppages; and other matters.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to continue as the company invests in manufacturing and technology.

evidence.text: We anticipate, and have prepared for, the majority of our arrangements in both reportable segments to be offered in similar subscription-type offerings over the coming years.

factors.summary: The company does not have long-term debt and has a revolving credit facility available, indicating a strong balance sheet with manageable leverage.

evidence.text: Based on our strong balance sheet and the fact that we do not have long-term debt at March 31, 2019, we believe financing will be available, both through our existing credit line and possible additional financing.

factors.summary: Free cash flow decreased significantly from the previous quarter, indicating potential liquidity challenges.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by (used in) operating activities $ (13,970 ) $ 18,044 Net cash provided by (used in) investing activities (110,755 ) 1,268 Net cash used in financing activities (1,159 ) (3,421 ) Effect of exchange rate changes on cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash 67 469 Net increase (decrease) in cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash $ (125,817 ) $ 16,360

factors.summary: The company is focused on organic growth rather than acquisition activity.

evidence.text: There is no mention of specific acquisitions or divestitures in the provided text.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential disruptions in the supply chain and geopolitical factors affecting sales and costs.

evidence.text: Higher costs or unavailability of materials could adversely affect our financial results. International or domestic geopolitical or other events, including the imposition of new or increased tariffs and/or quotas by the U.S. government on any of these raw materials or components, could adversely impact the supply and cost of these raw materials or components, and could adversely impact the profitability of our operations.

factors.summary: The supply chain is exposed to risks such as raw material shortages and transportation disruptions, indicating potential health issues.

evidence.text: Our reliance on third-party suppliers creates risks related to our potential inability to obtain an adequate supply of components or sub-assemblies and reduced control over pricing and timing of delivery of components and sub-assemblies.

factors.summary: The company is involved in product litigation and other legal proceedings, which could impact financial and operational performance.

evidence.text: The discussion under the headings Product Litigation, Other Litigation, and Federal Trade Commission Investigation in Note 12 of the notes to our condensed consolidated financial statements included in PART I, ITEM 1 of this Report on Form 10-Q is incorporated by reference herein.
